block_00: Take a look at the top news of the week
block_03: The pandemic is finally hitting big tech — two years later. Microsoft made headlines this week when reports emerged that it slashed nearly 1,000 jobs, making it the latest tech giant to respond to economic turmoil. The company characterized its layoffs as structural adjustments, stressing that it would keep hiring in key growth areas.
block_07: The share price rose by 25% in one week following the company's huge Q3 earnings report. The platform delivered a beat on both the top and bottom lines, in addition to 2.41M net subscriber additions — crushing estimates of 1M.
block_10: The company cut its full-year sales outlook. Due to economic uncertainty and foreign exchange headwinds, it expects net revenue to be no more than $425M instead of $470M. The share fell by 7% this week.
block_14: Stay tuned for emails, so you don't miss all the updates that are coming this year.
